claims.caveat: This checks search execution, not global optimality.
claims.claim: The morphology optimizer evaluated a non-trivial candidate set.

claims.caveat: Feasibility is defined by the implemented simulation constraints.
claims.claim: The search found at least one feasible morphology design.

claims.caveat: Selection depends on the configured cost and constraint model.
claims.claim: A best morphology design is explicitly selected.

claims.caveat: Held-out success is bounded to the encoded object/task protocol.
claims.claim: The selected design preserves full held-out task success.

claims.caveat: Reach margin is an equivalent morphology proxy, not hardware clearance.
claims.claim: The selected design satisfies the required robust reach margin.

claims.caveat: The metric is computed on Panda replay states in simulation.
claims.claim: The selected design satisfies the minimum Jacobian singular-value threshold.

claims.caveat: Condition number is a kinematic quality metric, not a force guarantee.
claims.claim: The selected design satisfies the maximum Jacobian condition-number threshold.

claims.caveat: The count should be interpreted with the listed perturbation settings.
claims.claim: A non-empty robustness benchmark was executed.

claims.caveat: This does not imply unbounded robustness outside the perturbation radius.
claims.claim: The final robustness benchmark has no failed runs.

claims.caveat: Only failures recorded by the current evaluator are counted.
claims.claim: Failure analysis reports zero failed robustness entries.

claims.caveat: No hardware, force-closure, tactile, actuator-torque, or sim-to-real evidence is present; this remains a simulation claim.
claims.claim: The results prove real-world hardware or manufacturable Panda redesign performance.
